ref: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
text: Keŋte be gyuuwɔ Kapanum donɔɔ man. Te Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee kelii nɛ, Yesu gyuuwɔ bɔ ɔsom deni man ɔ kaa wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia.
translated: Keŋte went to the people of Kapanum. And the Gyudatena spoke to them, saying, "Jesus came to the son of man, and he is the one who is the Word of Life in Wurubuarɛ."

ref: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
text: Botɔɔ te maa yako baŋ be kyaa ma demena dɔɔ nɛ nyiaa, ‘Ɛ seŋ lee me gyaŋ, ɛmɛɛ baŋ Wurubuarɛ be falaa ŋon nyi ya gyu ka gyoo boalaŋ degyapaa keŋ ɔ be desina see fa ɔbɔnsam na ɔ kpilala baŋ nɛ man.
translated: Botɔɔ te maa yako baŋ be kyaa ma demena dɔɔ nɛ nyiaa, ‘Ɛ seŋ lee me gyaŋ, ɛmɛɛ baŋ Wurubuarɛ be falaa ŋon nyi ya gyu ka gyoo boalaŋ degyapaa keŋ ɔ be desina see fa ɔbɔnsam na ɔ kpilala baŋ nɛ man.

ref: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
text: Botɔɔ te ba moo tee ŋaŋte daale mo kyakale na soloŋ fa Yesu nyi waa nyɔɔ. Mɔna ɔ ke daa ke kɛɛ nɛ te ɔ bɛɛwɔ nyi ɔ be nyɔɔ ya.
translated: Boto went to the time of the month when he arrived at the entrance of the baptism of Jesus, whose name is. Then he went to the place where he called them by their names.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: Akpelee koya yɛɛ aŋmoŋ? Na kaperɛ te bɛɛ yɔɔ akpeleese bala yee? Mɔna akpaa Wurubuarɛ te fa gbɛɛ ya na, be man dokoloŋ gba be gyae waa yala yeŋ ya.
translated: Why are you not coming here? And if you don't want to speak, then why are you talking? Then go to Wurubuarɛ and tell him, he will listen and give you the word of your choice.

ref: Simon was one of the twelve, and Jesus named him Peter.
text: Baala kufu bala baŋ Yesu ba lese nɛ yela yaa, Simɔn te ɔ doo e yele nyi Pita, na
translated: The ones who followed Jesus saw him going there, Simon, who was called Peter, and

ref: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
text: Keŋte feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan waase Yesu nyiaa, “Akpaa n ke gegi daa na, yela de kaa gyoo kekuiriise dikpii kei man.”
translated: The name of the word of Jesus says, "My father is your father, and you are the heavenly kingdom of man."

ref: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:
text: Donɔɔse ŋan man Yesu be yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia nɛ, ŋe man balaŋ burum te lɛɛ e di na baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man, na baa kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya. Keŋte Yesu kolosiwɔ tia wɔ nyiaa,
translated: The man Donose, whose name is Jesus, was healed. And the man was carried by people, and he was able to walk, and they praised loudly, and they believed in the man. And they went with Wurubuarɛ to be healed. But Jesus preached to them, and they listened.

ref: The girl hurried back and told Herod, “Here and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Debokenaŋ man te alebu ŋon barasewɔ gyu gyoo Herode gyaŋ ke yako e nyiaa, “Mɛɛ gyae n ke kara Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre nyee do tasa taŋtaŋalaŋ man fa maŋ.”
translated: The people of the land, when they spoke to Herod, said, "Our ancestors from long ago, when the earth was created, said to us that the people of the land must not..."

ref: Then his disciples came over to him and asked, “Do you know you insulted the Pharisees by what you said?”
text: Keŋte Yesu kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse nyiaa, “Ŋgba n gyeŋ nyi debaŋ keŋ Farasiitena baŋ be nyii nombia ŋan na, te yɛɛ wɔ gyoŋ ya?”
translated: Jesus said to the Pharisees, "Why do you not wash your hands, or are you hypocrites?"

ref: Some Pharisees noticed this and said to Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do this!”
text: Farasiitena baŋ kena mena nɛ be yako Yesu nyiaa, “Kɛɛ! N kaseela ne yɛɛ kpene keŋ da mmaraa bɛɛ fa gbɛɛ nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ da kefɛɛfowee ya.”
translated: The Pharisees came to him and said to Jesus, "Ah! You are a scribe, you have interpreted the law. Why do you transgress the commandments of the elders and make void the commandments of God?"

ref: Haven't you read in the Law of Moses that the priests are allowed to work in the temple on the Sabbath? But no one says they are guilty of breaking the law of the Sabbath.
text: Yaa ɛ ta bese ta kala lee Wurubuarɛ mmaraa tɔne keŋ man nyi, saese baŋ be kyaa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ, deesiwɔ fa Wurubuarɛ kefɛɛfowee keŋ mɔna ŋolo te bu wɔ fɔɔ nyi ba te walaa ke yee?
translated: Hey, you are going to Wurubuarɛ's council meeting with the people. You are the one who knows the truth of the matter among them. How can we achieve this with the wisdom of the elders so that we can walk together without conflict?

ref: Tell us what you think! Should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?”
text: Mena dɔɔ yako daa nya agyueŋ. Dei nyi dɛɛ tɔ lempoo fa Romantena gyoo dinaa ŋon, yaa de na tɔɔ ya?”
translated: "Mena, come here, my friend. Why are you sitting there with Romantena doing nothing, or are you going to do something?"

ref: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
text: Akpaa nyi Bɛlisɛbu doŋ te mɛɛ mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ na, amɔte doŋ yaa ɛ balaŋ baŋ mɔ na mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ? Mena na ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ balaŋ ne gyae la baa bu ŋon fɔɔ.
translated: Akpaa Nyi went to Bɛlisɛbu with his family to feliŋkumɛɛ, but where did he go to the village of his people? However, he arrived at the village and saw that there were many people.

ref: But Jesus told him, “Put your sword away. Anyone who lives by fighting will die by fighting.
text: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseele ŋon nyiaa, “Mo n yoosila keŋ do ke bɔɔlee man. Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne wɔŋ yoosila na, waa yeŋ bo yoosila nɔɔman.
translated: The Lord Jesus said, "I came with my disciples to heal the sick. O Nazareth, you who are from the village, come to me, or heal the rest of the people."

ref: And you ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching. You do a lot of other things just as bad. What Really Makes People Unclean (Matthew
text: Mena yaa ɛ na mo ɛ beneŋ amanberɛse ŋan wɔlɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan, te ɛ yɛɛ ŋa see ɛ bia mɔ. Te ɛ ne yɛɛ nombia burum ŋan ŋe yɛɛ ŋgba kɛŋa dinɔɔ nɛ kpu.” Kpene keŋ ne yɛɛ walaŋ ayimɛɛ Mateo
translated: "Mena said that he is the one who created the world, and he said that he is the one who created the earth and the sky above. And the one who wrote the Gospel according to Matthew."

ref: When did we welcome you as a stranger or give you clothes to wear
text: Woromɔ te de naa neŋ nyi n yɛɛ ŋɔɔle te de lɛɛ neŋ gyu da akpaŋalaŋse man, yaa n dɔŋɛɛ diteŋteli te de faa neŋ abɔɔ n doowɔ?
translated: How do you know that the world is the time to come together, and how do you prepare to do the work well?

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
text: Botɔɔ te alebu ŋon leewɔ yenaŋ gyu ke bɔɔse ɔ naa nyiaa, “Nna, woŋ te maa bɔɔse?” Botɔɔ te ɔ naa yako e nyiaa, “Yako nyi n ne gyae Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon nyee.”
translated: Botɔɔ asked the people about the matter of the child and said, "Indeed, is your child?" Botɔɔ replied and said, "I trust in Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ, the living among the living."

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: “Koro na n ka mo bu ŋon na ɔ naa, na ɛ ka bese gyu Iserae tɛɛle man. Nawolo nyi balaŋ baŋ nafɔ bɛɛ gyae bu ŋon baa ko nɛ ta yekee.”
translated: Koro, I have your father and he is here; and he will take you to Israel's land. Nawolo, my brothers will fight with your father and your brothers until you return.

ref: After Jesus had forced the demon out, the man started talking. The crowds were so amazed they began saying, “Nothing like this has ever happened in Israel!”
text: Yesu ke gegi feliŋkum keŋ lee balee ŋon man nɛ, te ɔ kolosiwɔ. Balaŋ dikpii keŋ kena mena nɛ, nombia ŋan doo be nɔɔ te be yakowɔ nyiaa, “Da te naa ta mena nombia kɛŋa see Iserae tɛɛle kei dɔɔ ya.”
translated: Jesus came with many companions and healed them. The sick people there said to him, "This is the one who heals Israel's people, glorify God."

ref: After Jesus had made the Sadducees look foolish, the Pharisees heard about it and got together.
text: Farasiitena baŋ ke nyii nyi Yesu ta mo ɔ nyansa di Sadukiitena baŋ dɔɔ nɛ, be popou kaa gyaŋeewɔ debodokoloŋ.
translated: The Pharisees and others say the words of Jesus, and they debate with the Sadducees, but the people hear the words of the kingdom.

ref: His son told him he would not do it, but later he changed his mind and went.
text: Keŋte ɔ bu ŋon yako e nyiaa, mɔɔ gyae maa gyu ya, mɔna wɔle wɔle nɛ, ɔ kaa kyɛɛkee wa agyueŋ te ɔ gyuuwɔ.
translated: He is going to eat my food and your food, then he will take my children. And they are coming. He will prepare the meal and invite them.

ref: He then begged Jesus not to send them away.
text: Keŋte balee ŋon waase Yesu agbaatee agbaatee nyi, ɔ na gegi feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan lee depuŋ kenaŋ man ya.
translated: Follow those who call on the name of Jesus step by step, and he goes with us among the people of the land.

ref: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
text: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ta yoo, nawolo nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ya, te ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ doŋ mɔ ya.

ref: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
text: Mɔna ɛ gyae abɔɔ see ɛ wose Wurubuarɛ man, botɔɔ keŋ paapaase na kandorɔse be wɔlɛɛ ŋa ya, te ŋmɛɛlatena mɔ be gyae baa tale gyu ke ŋmɛɛle ŋa ya nɛ.
translated: Mona has gone to see the Wurubuarɛ people, they have prepared the documents and the ceremonies for the elders, and the leaders have decided that they will proceed with the meeting as planned.

ref: Jesus then told them this story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a farmer scattered good seed in a field.
text: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu wɔle fa wɔ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ balee ŋolo be gyu ke ŋmatee abɔɔ bia kpaakpaa nyiŋan ɔ wɔɔman nɛ.
translated: Jesus said to the people, "The Kingdom of Heaven is near. Now the time has come; repent of your sins and believe in the good news of the kingdom that I bring to you all." Amen.
